member.name: T:PrivateKey
member.summary: A private key on the Hedera™ network.

member.name: F:PrivateKey.publicKey
member.summary: The public key derived from the private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.Generate
member.summary: Generates a new Ed25519 private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.GenerateED25519
member.summary: Extract the new ED25519 private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.GenerateECDSA
member.summary: Extract the new ECDSA private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromSeedED25519(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Extract the ED25519 private key from a seed.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromSeedECDSAsecp256k1(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Extract the ECDSA private key from a seed.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromMnemonic(Mnemonic,System.String)
member.summary:
the mnemonic phrase which should be a 24 byte list of words. the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic (not used in the mobile wallets, use {@link #fromMnemonic(Mnemonic)} instead.) the recovered key; use {@link #derive(int)} to get a key for an account index (0 for default account) @deprecateduse {@link Mnemonic#toStandardEd25519PrivateKey(String, int)} ()} or {@link Mnemonic#toStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKey(String, int)} (String, int)} instead This function uses incomplete and the key should not be used directly.

Recover a private key from a generated mnemonic phrase and a passphrase.

This is not compatible with the phrases generated by the Android and iOS wallets; use the no-passphrase version instead.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromMnemonic(Mnemonic)
member.summary:
the mnemonic phrase which should be a 24 byte list of words. the recovered key; use {@link #derive(int)} to get a key for an account index (0 for default account) @deprecateduse {@link Mnemonic#toStandardEd25519PrivateKey(String, int)} ()} or {@link Mnemonic#toStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKey(String, int)} (String, int)} instead Recover a private key from a mnemonic phrase compatible with the iOS and Android wallets.

An overload of {@link #fromMnemonic(Mnemonic, String)} which uses an empty string for the passphrase.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromString(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from a string.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromStringDER(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from a DER encoded string.
member.param: DER encoded string representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromStringED25519(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from an ED25519 encoded string.
member.param: ED25519 encoded string representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromStringECDSA(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from an ECDSA encoded string.
member.param: ECDSA encoded string representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromBytes(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from a byte array.
member.param: byte array representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromBytesED25519(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from an ED25519 encoded byte array.
member.param: ED25519 encoded byte array representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromBytesECDSA(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from an ECDSA encoded byte array.
member.param: ECDSA encoded byte array representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromBytesDER(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from a DER encoded byte array.
member.param: DER encoded byte array representing a private key

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromPrivateKeyInfo(PrivateKeyInfo)
member.summary: Retrieve a private key from a private key info object.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ReadPem(PemReader)
member.summary:
Parse a private key from a PEM encoded reader.

This will read the first "PRIVATE KEY" section in the stream as an Ed25519 private key.

The Reader containing the pem file {@link PrivateKey} if one occurred while reading. if no "PRIVATE KEY" section was found or the key was not an Ed25519 private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ReadPem(PemReader,System.String)
member.summary:
Parse a private key from a PEM encoded stream. The key may be encrypted, e.g. if it was generated by OpenSSL.

If password is not null or empty, this will read the first "ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY" section in the stream as a PKCS#8 EncryptedPrivateKeyInfo structure and use that algorithm to decrypt the private key with the given password. Otherwise, it will read the first "PRIVATE KEY" section as DER-encoded Ed25519 private key.

To generate an encrypted private key with OpenSSL, open a terminal and enter the following command: 

    {@code openssl genpkey -algorithm ed25519 -aes-128-cbc > key.pem}

Then enter your password of choice when prompted. When the command completes, your encrypted key will be saved as `key.pem` in the working directory of your terminal.

the PEM encoded file the password to decrypt the PEM file; if null or empty, no decryption is performed. {@link PrivateKey} if one occurred while reading the PEM file if no "ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY" or "PRIVATE KEY" section was found, if the passphrase is wrong or the key was not an Ed25519 private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromPem(System.String)
member.summary: Parse a private key from a PEM encoded string.
member.exception: if the PEM string was improperly encoded
if no "PRIVATE KEY" section was found or the key was not an Ed25519 private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.FromPem(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Parse a private key from a PEM encoded string.

The private key may be encrypted, e.g. if it was generated by OpenSSL.

the encoded PEM string the password to decrypt the PEM file; if null or empty, no decryption is performed. {@link PrivateKey} if the PEM string was improperly encoded if no "ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY" or "PRIVATE KEY" section was found, if the passphrase is wrong or the key was not an Ed25519 private key. @see#readPem(Reader, String)

member.name: M:PrivateKey.LegacyDerive(System.Int32)
member.summary: Derive a child key based on the index.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.LegacyDerive(System.Int64)
member.summary: Derive a child key based on the index.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.IsDerivable
member.summary:
Check if this private key supports derivation.

This is currently only the case if this private key was created from a mnemonic.

bool

member.name: M:PrivateKey.Derive(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Given a wallet/account index, derive a child key compatible with the iOS and Android wallets.

Use index 0 for the default account.

the wallet/account index of the account, 0 for the default account. the derived key if this key does not support derivation. @see#isDerivable()

member.name: M:PrivateKey.GetPublicKey
member.summary:
Derive a public key from this private key.

The public key can be freely given and used by other parties to verify the signatures generated by this private key.

the corresponding public key for this private key.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.Sign(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Sign a message with this private key.
member.param: The array of bytes to sign with

member.name: M:PrivateKey.SignTransaction``1(Transaction{``0})
member.summary: Sign a transaction.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ToBytesDER
member.summary: Extract the byte array encoded as DER.
member.returns: the byte array encoded as DER

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ToBytesRaw
member.summary: Extract the raw byte array.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ToStringDER
member.summary: Extract the DER encoded hex string.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ToStringRaw
member.summary: Extract the raw hex string.

member.name: M:PrivateKey.ToAccountId(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Retrieve the account id.

member.summary: Are we an ED25519 key?
